title: solanum corymbiflorum subsp. mortonianum
intro: Solanum corymbiflorum subsp. mortonianum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the nightshade family, Solanaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to Western Australia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The plant is valued for its leaves, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Solanum corymbiflorum subsp. mortonianum include teas and infusions made from the leaves, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
